governing_rule: A source entry records what a work can support and what it cannot establish. Registry inclusion is not endorsement of the source's theory, and a source may not be cited beyond its recorded evidential scope.
claim_backlink_policy: The claims array is a generated index of claims reached through ARGUMENT_GRAPH.json propositions. It records use, not support: evidential direction and strength remain authoritative only on typed graph edges.

sources.title: Towards a true neural stance on consciousness
supports: Recurrent processing theory assigns a central consciousness-related role to recurrent neural interactions rather than a purely feed-forward sweep.
does_not_establish: That recurrence is sufficient for consciousness.
That generic iterative software computation instantiates the theory-relevant neural mechanism.

sources.title: The distinct modes of vision offered by feedforward and recurrent processing
supports: Feed-forward and recurrent visual processing can be theoretically and empirically distinguished.
does_not_establish: That every recurrent system is conscious.
That recurrent processing alone creates a unified subject.

sources.title: A Cognitive Theory of Consciousness
supports: Global workspace theory models conscious access through broad availability of selected information to specialized processes.
does_not_establish: That software message passing is conscious.
That global availability is phenomenally sufficient.

sources.title: A neuronal network model linking subjective reports and objective physiological data during conscious perception
supports: Global neuronal workspace models connect conscious access with nonlinear amplification and broad neural availability.
does_not_establish: That the modeled signatures are theory-neutral markers of phenomenal consciousness.
That analogous output behavior in an AI system preserves the relevant mechanism.

sources.title: Experimental and theoretical approaches to conscious processing
supports: Global neuronal workspace theory relates conscious processing to large-scale access, recurrent amplification, and report-relevant availability.
does_not_establish: That reportability and phenomenality are identical.
That GNWT has been decisively validated over rival theories.

sources.title: Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness
supports: A preregistered adversarial collaboration directly compared predictions associated with GNWT and IIT and produced a mixed evidential pattern.
does_not_establish: That either GNWT or IIT was decisively validated or falsified as a complete theory.
That the tested neural signatures are sufficient for consciousness in artificial systems.
sources.verification_note: Official Nature article metadata and DOI were independently checked. Proposition fit and inferential reach remain controlled separately by ARGUMENT_GRAPH.json.

sources.title: Empirical support for higher-order theories of conscious awareness
supports: Higher-order theories connect conscious awareness to representations of first-order mental states.
does_not_establish: That metacognitive language is sufficient evidence of experience.
That a post-hoc self-report generator implements a higher-order consciousness mechanism.

sources.title: The free-energy principle: a unified brain theory?
supports: The free-energy principle presents perception, learning, and action within a generative-model and prediction-error framework.
does_not_establish: That prediction error is pain.
That predictive processing by itself is a sufficient theory or test of consciousness.

sources.title: Whatever next? Predictive brains, situated agents, and the future of cognitive science
supports: Predictive-processing approaches characterize cognition through hierarchical prediction, error correction, and situated action.
does_not_establish: That next-token prediction is equivalent to conscious predictive processing.
That prediction is phenomenally sufficient.

sources.title: Interoceptive inference, emotion, and the embodied self
supports: Interoceptive predictive inference has been proposed as relevant to emotion and embodied selfhood.
does_not_establish: That any homeostatic variable is felt valence.
That a software self-model has an embodied phenomenal self.

sources.title: The attention schema theory: a mechanistic account of subjective awareness
supports: Attention schema theory proposes that a simplified model of attention supports awareness attribution and attentional control.
does_not_establish: That explaining awareness reports settles whether experience exists.
That first-person language alone evidences an attention schema.

sources.title: Integrated information theory (IIT) 4.0: Formulating the properties of phenomenal existence in physical terms
supports: IIT 4.0 identifies experience with a maximally irreducible intrinsic cause-effect structure under its formalism.
does_not_establish: That generic complexity, entropy, connectivity, mutual information, or an IIT-inspired proxy equals integrated information under IIT.
That IIT's axioms or identity claim are empirically settled.

sources.title: Minds, brains, and programs
supports: Searle argues that formal symbol manipulation is not sufficient for understanding or intentionality.
does_not_establish: That all nonbiological systems are unconscious.
Which concrete biological causal powers are necessary for consciousness.

sources.title: The Embodied Mind
supports: Enactive and embodied approaches reject the view that cognition is exhausted by detached internal representation.
does_not_establish: That embodiment alone is sufficient for consciousness.
That attaching sensors or a robot body creates a subject.

sources.title: Illusionism as a theory of consciousness
supports: Illusionism treats introspective judgments about phenomenal properties as targets for mechanistic explanation.
does_not_establish: That explaining consciousness reports is universally accepted as explaining consciousness.
That an artificial system with introspective distortions is conscious or unconscious.
